top_papers.figure_caption: Example typographic prompt injection attack using the TAP jailbreak method. (a) Harmful text prompt requesting attack planning guidance. (b) Prompt rendered as a 20px typographic image. (c) 30° rotation transformation increases L2 distance from text embedding.

top_papers.title: Redefining Quality Criteria and Distance-Aware Score Modeling for Image Editing Assessment
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **DS-IEQA**, a unified framework for Image Editing Quality Assessment that overcomes limitations in heuristic prompting and distance-agnostic scoring of existing MLLM methods. It achieves this through **Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO)** to automatically refine evaluation criteria and **Token-Decoupled Distance Regression Loss (TDRL)** to explicitly model the continuous structure of quality scores. The core contribution is a method that jointly learns evaluation criteria and score representations for more accurate and human-aligned IEQA.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Feedback-Driven Metric Prompt Optimization (FDMPO). The framework iteratively refines metric definitions via feedback signals to better align with implicit human evaluation criteria.

top_papers.title: ReflectCAP: Detailed Image Captioning with Reflective Memory
top_papers.abstract: ReflectCAP introduces a multi-agent pipeline to analyze a target Large Vision-Language Model's (LVLM) systematic errors (hallucinations and oversights) and distill these findings into **Structured Reflection Notes**. These notes then guide the captioning model at inference time, steering it to **avoid known inaccuracies while focusing on necessary details**, thereby improving both factuality and fine-grained coverage simultaneously. This method achieves a Pareto-optimal balance between these two metrics across various LVLMs and shows significant gains on automated caption evaluation benchmarks.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of ReflectCAP. In the offline phase, a multi-agent reflective learning pipeline distills a target LVLM’s recurring captioning errors and omissions into Structured Reflection Notes. In the online phase, these notes guide caption generation for new images, producing captions that better balance factuality and coverage.

top_papers.title: Ride the Wave: Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention for Smooth Video Generation
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces Precision-Allocated Sparse Attention (PASA) to reduce the computational cost of Video Diffusion Transformers while eliminating flickering artifacts. PASA uses a curvature-aware dynamic budgeting mechanism to elastically allocate computation only during critical semantic transitions. This, combined with hardware-aligned grouped approximations, achieves efficient and temporally smooth high-fidelity video generation without retraining.
top_papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . Visual and quantitative comparison on Wan 2.1-T2V-1.3B (quality vs. efficiency). PASA (ours) matches the end-to-end speedup of PISA ( Piecewise Sparse Attention ) while improving PSNR over other sparse attention baselines. Left: PASA preserves continuous strap geometry; other sparse methods show discontinuities. Right: PASA avoids the anatomical distortion present in competing sparse methods.

top_papers.title: SceneCritic: A Symbolic Evaluator for 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis
top_papers.abstract: SceneCritic is a novel symbolic evaluator designed to assess the spatial plausibility of 3D indoor scenes generated via LLMs/VLMs, moving beyond subjective rendered-view judgments. It achieves this by grounding its verification logic in SceneOnto, a structured spatial ontology built from existing 3D datasets. SceneCritic provides objective, object-level and relationship-level assessments of semantic, orientation, and geometric coherence, offering specific feedback on scene violations.
top_papers.figure_caption: VLM Evaluation vs. SceneCritic . Given a piano room with nine objects, the VLM assigns different scores to the same scene depending on which view is rendered, while SceneCritic evaluates the layout directly by traversing relational constraints from SceneOnto (e.g., incorrect table scales, chairs not facing the table they co-occur with). This produces stable, object-level assessments that identify specific violations alongside successful placements. SceneCritic scores also align more closely with human preferences.

top_papers.title: T2I-BiasBench: A Multi-Metric Framework for Auditing Demographic and Cultural Bias in Text-to-Image Models
top_papers.abstract: T2I-BiasBench is a novel, unified evaluation framework designed to audit Text-to-Image models for demographic bias, element omission, and cultural collapse simultaneously, using thirteen complementary metrics. The paper introduces seven new or adapted metrics to capture these three dimensions comprehensively across five prompt categories. This work provides the first framework to jointly assess these critical biases in diffusion models, benchmarking three open-source models against a reference baseline.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of the proposed Bias and Fairness Evaluation Framework for text-to-image models. The pipeline consists of prompt generation (demographic and contextual), controlled image synthesis across multiple models, attribute extraction via vision-language methods, and a multi-metric evaluation module comprising 13 fairness, diversity, and alignment metrics. The framework produces composite bias and diversity scores, enabling comparative analysis of model behavior across demographic attributes and contextual scenarios.

top_papers.title: Task Alignment: A simple and effective proxy for model merging in computer vision
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Task Alignment** as a simple and effective proxy to accelerate hyperparameter selection for model merging in computer vision, especially when models have trainable, heterogeneous decoders. By using this proxy instead of costly downstream evaluation, the method significantly speeds up the process of merging models fine-tuned on different tasks while maintaining performance.
top_papers.figure_caption: (Left) Model merging methods produce a large number of candidates which depend on hyperparameters ( λ , μ ) (\( \lambda \),\( \mu \)) . (Middle) SOTA methods select hyperparameters based on validation performance. This is efficient for frozen decoders like CLIP (standard benchmarks) but becomes infeasible for more complex sets of tasks that require fine-tuning multiple task-specific decoders, for each hyperparameter candidate . (Right) Our proposed Task Alignment Proxy (TAP) can be used to select hyperparameters at a fraction of the cost while maintaining performance (see also Fig. ˜ 2 ).

top_papers.title: Towards Realistic and Consistent Orbital Video Generation via 3D Foundation Priors
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces a method for generating geometrically realistic and consistent orbital videos from a single image by incorporating 3D shape priors. The core method leverages a 3D foundational generative model to provide structural guidance through global latent vectors and projected latent images, overcoming the limitations of pixel-wise attention for long-range view extrapolation. This approach ensures the generated videos maintain a plausible and coherent object structure throughout the orbit.

top_papers.title: SOAR: Self-Correction for Optimal Alignment and Refinement in Diffusion Models
top_papers.abstract: SOAR addresses the exposure bias in diffusion model post-training by introducing a self-correction mechanism that bridges the gap between supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning (RL). The core method involves performing a single, stop-gradient rollout from a real sample, re-noising the resulting intermediate state, and then using this newly generated noisy state as a target for the model to predict the original noise, effectively training the model to correct its own trajectory errors. This contributes a novel, efficient bias-correction technique that refines alignment without relying on sparse terminal rewards.
top_papers.figure_caption: Conceptual comparison of post-training regimes from a trajectory perspective. Left: SFT trains only on ground-truth denoising states. Once an inference rollout exits the narrow corridor of well-optimized states, subsequent steps sharpen the wrong structure. Middle: RL separates good and bad final outcomes with a terminal reward, but the sparse signal arrives after the erroneous rollout has already formed. Right: SOAR performs a one-step rollout to generate off-trajectory states and supervises the model to correct back toward the clean target, acting before semantic errors fully materialize.

top_papers.title: A Multi-Stage Optimization Pipeline for Bethesda Cell Detection in Pap Smear Cytology
top_papers.abstract: This paper presents a multi-stage optimization pipeline for detecting Bethesda cells in Pap smear images, developed for the ISBI Riva Cytology Challenge. The core method combines an ensemble of YOLO and U-Net architectures for initial detection, followed by a refinement stage using overlap removal and a binary classifier. This framework achieved a strong second-place finish in the competition with a mAP50-95 score of 0.5909.

top_papers.title: A Multimodal Clinically Informed Coarse-to-Fine Framework for Longitudinal CT Registration in Proton Therapy
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces a clinically scalable, coarse-to-fine framework for longitudinal CT registration in proton therapy, addressing the speed and clinical relevance limitations of existing methods. The model uses dual CNN encoders and a transformer decoder to progressively refine deformation fields, crucially incorporating multimodal clinical priors like contours, dose, and planning text to guide the registration process. This integration of clinical context enhances accuracy and robustness for time-sensitive adaptive radiotherapy workflows.

top_papers.title: DiffMagicFace: Identity Consistent Facial Editing of Real Videos
top_papers.abstract: DiffMagicFace is a novel video editing framework that uses two concurrently operating, fine-tuned diffusion models to achieve text-conditioned facial video editing. Its core contribution is ensuring strong identity preservation and cross-frame consistency in the edited video, achieved partly through a specially constructed dataset of varied facial perspectives for each subject. This method delivers high-quality results without relying on pre-existing video datasets.
top_papers.figure_caption: Identity consistent face video editing. Our method achieves face video editing on a specific subject (e.g., half-frame glasses, or fat facial effect) using a text prompt, while preserving the identity of the source video and maintaining high consistency among frames.

top_papers.title: Efficient Multi-View 3D Object Detection by Dynamic Token Selection and Fine-Tuning
top_papers.abstract: This paper proposes a method to efficiently accelerate multi-view 3D object detection using Vision Transformers (ViTs) by introducing **dynamic layer-wise token selection** within the backbone, overcoming the fixed ratios of previous work. Additionally, it employs a **parameter-efficient fine-tuning strategy** instead of full retraining. This combination significantly improves computational efficiency during both training and inference for 3D object detection tasks.

top_papers.title: Enhanced Text-to-Image Generation by Fine-grained Multimodal Reasoning
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Fine-grained Multimodal Reasoning (FiMR)**, a framework that enhances text-to-image generation by leveraging the reasoning capabilities of unified MLLMs. FiMR decomposes input prompts into minimal semantic units (entities/attributes) and uses decomposed Visual Question Answering (VQA) to generate explicit, fine-grained feedback. This feedback enables targeted, localized refinements, overcoming the limitations of existing methods that rely only on holistic alignment judgments.
top_papers.figure_caption: An example of the text-to-image reasoning process of Janus-Pro-R1.

top_papers.title: Feed-Forward 3D Scene Modeling: A Problem-Driven Perspective
top_papers.abstract: This paper surveys feed-forward methods for 3D scene modeling, which aim to reconstruct 3D representations directly from 2D inputs in a single forward pass, overcoming the limitations of slow optimization or category-specific training. The core contribution is a problem-driven perspective that unifies these diverse approaches by identifying shared high-level architectural patterns, such as feature extraction, multi-view fusion, and geometry-aware decoders, across various output representations. This framework helps understand the current landscape and guides future research toward more generalizable and efficient 3D reconstruction.
top_papers.figure_caption: Outline of the survey. The paper begins with an overview of core 3D representations (NeRF [ 1 ] , 3D Gaussian Splatting [ 2 ] , Pointmap [ 3 ] , and others [ 4 , 5 , 6 , 7 , 8 ] ) and how feed-forward networks generate them. It then analyzes methods along five challenge-driven axes: feature enhancement, geometry awareness, model efficiency, data or visual augmentation, and temporal modeling, followed by reviews of datasets and benchmarks, practical applications ( e.g. , autonomous driving and robotics). Finally, based on a statistical summary of the surveyed works, we outline recent trends and discuss future directions for feed-forward 3D scene modeling.

top_papers.title: Free Geometry: Refining 3D Reconstruction from Longer Versions of Itself
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Free Geometry**, a novel framework that allows efficient, feed-forward 3D reconstruction models to self-refine their output during testing without needing 3D ground truth. The core method leverages the increased reliability of reconstructions generated from longer input sequences by enforcing cross-view feature consistency between full and partially masked observations. This self-supervision enables fast, lightweight recalibration (via LoRA updates) to correct errors arising from ambiguities like occlusion.
top_papers.figure_caption: Free Geometry enables feed-forward 3D reconstruction models to self-evolve at test time without any 3D ground truth and generalize on models and datasets.

top_papers.title: Indexing Multimodal Language Models for Large-scale Image Retrieval
top_papers.abstract: This paper proposes using pre-trained Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) as training-free similarity estimators for instance-level image retrieval. The core method involves prompting the MLLM with paired images and converting next-token probabilities into similarity scores for zero-shot re-ranking. This approach leverages the MLLMs' inherent visual knowledge without fine-tuning, demonstrating superior robustness compared to specialized re-rankers in large-scale retrieval pipelines.

top_papers.title: Med-CAM: Minimal Evidence for Explaining Medical Decision Making
top_papers.abstract: Med-CAM introduces a novel framework to generate **minimal and sharp segmentation masks** as evidence for medical AI decisions, addressing the opacity of black-box models. It trains a dedicated segmentation network to highlight only the **critical, minimal evidence** necessary for the model's diagnosis. This approach provides explanations with superior spatial awareness compared to prior methods, making them more faithful and interpretable for clinicians.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of the Med-CAM framework. Given an input medical image x x , a lightweight U-Net produces a binary mask m m , yielding the masked explanation e = m ⊙ x e=m\( \odot \) x . The mask highlights the minimal diagnostic evidence required to preserve the model’s decision.

top_papers.title: One Token per Highly Selective Frame: Towards Extreme Compression for Long Video Understanding
top_papers.abstract: This paper proposes **LP-Comp (Learnable and Progressive Compression)** to address the token overload in long video understanding by aiming for one token per frame at the final LLM layer. Instead of heuristic methods, LP-Comp uses learnable, progressive modules for token-level compression, allowing the VLM to process significantly more frames with improved performance. The method is further enhanced by investigating frame-level compression based on internal LLM attention scores.
top_papers.figure_caption: Left: We present XComp that explores using the LLM layers to progressively compress the video tokens towards the extreme of one token per frame . Right: Such capabilities enable the model to better improve itself with denser input frames without significantly increasing video tokens.

top_papers.title: Reconstruction of a 3D wireframe from a single line drawing via generative depth estimation
top_papers.abstract: This paper proposes a novel generative method to reconstruct 3D wireframes from single 2D line drawings by framing it as a conditional dense depth estimation task. It utilizes a Latent Diffusion Model conditioned via a ControlNet-style framework to resolve projection ambiguities. The contribution lies in enabling flexible 3D reconstruction beyond rigid primitives, supporting iterative workflows through a BFS masking strategy for partial depth simulation.
top_papers.figure_caption: Line drawing reconstruction graphic. a) A wireframe sketch, in this case a cube, presents numerous “possible” reconstructions. b) Actual 2D input line drawing, predicted depth map, and 3D reconstruction output.

top_papers.title: Reward Design for Physical Reasoning in Vision-Language Models
top_papers.abstract: This paper investigates how different reward designs impact the physical reasoning capabilities of Vision-Language Models (VLMs) using a Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) framework. The core method involves a systematic ablation study comparing four reward signals, ranging from simple format compliance to a novel reward derived from the model's internal attention weights. The contribution is a comprehensive analysis demonstrating the effect of reward richness on VLM performance across diverse physics reasoning tasks.
top_papers.figure_caption: Reward design spectrum for GRPO-based VLM training on physical reasoning. Given a PhyX image-question pair, the model generates structured outputs and is trained with GRPO. We compare four rewards: (R1) format, (R2) accuracy, (R3) rubric (correctness, principle, unit), and (R4) attention-based visual grounding.

top_papers.title: ROSE: Retrieval-Oriented Segmentation Enhancement
top_papers.abstract: The paper introduces the Novel Emerging Segmentation Task (NEST) to address the challenge of segmenting new or emerging entities using multimodal large language models (MLLMs). The core method is **ROSE (Retrieval-Oriented Segmentation Enhancement)**, a plug-and-play framework that augments existing MLLM segmenters by integrating an Internet Retrieval-Augmented Generation module. This allows the model to access up-to-date external knowledge to accurately segment entities beyond its initial training data.

top_papers.title: SLQ: Bridging Modalities via Shared Latent Queries for Retrieval with Frozen MLLMs
top_papers.abstract: SLQ proposes an efficient method to adapt frozen Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) for retrieval without invasive fine-tuning. It introduces a small set of Shared Latent Queries appended to token sequences, which use the MLLM's native attention to generate compact, unified embeddings for both text and images. This approach elicits existing knowledge for retrieval while preserving the MLLM's core reasoning capabilities.

top_papers.title: Towards Unconstrained Human-Object Interaction
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces the **Unconstrained Human-Object Interaction (U-HOI)** task, removing the need for a predefined interaction vocabulary during training and inference. The core method leverages **Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs)** to recognize interactions from free-form text, employing a pipeline that converts the MLLM's textual output into structured interaction graphs. This work significantly contributes by enabling flexible, open-vocabulary HOI detection in real-world scenarios.
top_papers.figure_caption: Comparison between traditional HOI settings and our U-HOI. Unlike prior art, U-HOI does not require training and eliminates the need for a predefined list of interactions. We tackle U-HOI using off-the-shelf Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs), processing their free-form outputs and applying a post-generation refinement that extracts HOI triplets, all without being constrained by a fixed vocabulary.

top_papers.title: Training-Free Semantic Multi-Object Tracking with Vision-Language Models
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces TF-SMOT, a **training-free** pipeline for Semantic Multi-Object Tracking (SMOT) that leverages existing pretrained Vision-Language Models (VLMs). The core method **composes** components like SAM2 for tracking and InternVideo2.5 for captioning, using LLM-based semantic retrieval to generate human-interpretable outputs like summaries and interaction labels **without requiring end-to-end training**. This approach significantly improves adaptability by decoupling tracking progress from expensive, task-specific supervision.
top_papers.figure_caption: Comparison of training-based and training-free Semantic Multi-Object Tracking (SMOT) paradigms. Top: Traditional SMOT systems like SMOTer [ 16 ] require task-specific end-to-end training on annotated datasets, coupling tracking and semantic prediction to supervised learning. Bottom: our TF-SMOT operates entirely at inference time by composing frozen, pretrained components to produce tracking outputs together with video summaries, instance-level captions, and directed interactions, without using SMOT training data.

top_papers.title: What Are We Really Measuring? Rethinking Dataset Bias in Web-Scale Natural Image Collections via Unsupervised Semantic Clustering
top_papers.abstract: This paper challenges the standard method of measuring dataset bias by training classifiers, arguing that high accuracy often stems from resolution-based artifacts rather than true semantic differences. The authors demonstrate that these structural fingerprints, arising from native image resolutions and resizing, create robust, dataset-specific signatures that persist despite common augmentations. Their core contribution is showing that current bias metrics are frequently measuring these non-semantic artifacts, not meaningful semantic divergence.
top_papers.figure_caption: The ”Name That Dataset” game [ 36 ] aware of resolution distributions. These images are sampled from the YFCC and CC datasets. We maintain their relative sizes. Additionally, we provide a plot showing the resolution distribution of the training samples from each dataset. Can you guess which dataset each image is from? Once resolution effects are considered, dataset separability becomes significantly more apparent. (Answer: YFCC: 3, 5, 6, 8, 9; CC: 1, 2, 4, 7)

top_papers.title: Why MLLMs Struggle to Determine Object Orientations
top_papers.abstract: This paper investigates why Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) fail at 2D object orientation tasks by testing the hypothesis that visual encoders (like CLIP/SigLIP) lack geometric reasoning capabilities. The core method involves training linear regressors on encoder features (from models like LLaVA and Qwen) to predict object orientation, using both full images and rotated foreground patches. The contribution is the empirical finding that, contrary to the hypothesis, orientation information *is* preserved in the encoder embeddings, suggesting the failure lies elsewhere in the MLLM architecture.
top_papers.figure_caption: Set of images - Sets A and B are used for experiments with LLaVA-OV and Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct, and set C is used for LLaVA1.5 and LLaVA1.6. Since LLaVA-OV and Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct can be prompted with 2 images, real images were used. But since LLaVA1.5 and 1.6 can be prompted with only 1 image, synthetic images with reduced artifacts were used to improve the prediction accuracy. [A: Whole Images] : (a) dog (b) train (c) lizard (d) beach (e) indoor (f) fish. [B: In-Place Rotated Objects] : (a) koala on beach (b) vase on indoor (c) vase and toaster on indoor. [C: Blended Images with foreground on background] : (a) dog on beach (b) lizard on fish (c) train on indoor environment, with foreground scales 1, 2 and 3 from left to right.

top_papers.title: MAny: Merge Anything for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
top_papers.abstract: The paper introduces **MAny** (Merge Anything), a framework to address catastrophic forgetting in Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning (MCIT) by tackling dual forgetting in both perception and reasoning spaces. MAny achieves this through **Cross-modal Projection Merging (CPM)** to recover visual alignment via prototype guidance, and **Low-rank Parameter Merging (LPM)** to mitigate interference among task-specific parameters. This dual-merging approach effectively preserves past knowledge while adapting to new multimodal tasks.
top_papers.figure_caption: Performance diagnosis on UCIT benchmark for LLaVA-1.5-7B and InternVL-Chat-7B during sequential LoRA fine-tuning. Tasks follow a clockwise sequence from Image-R.

top_papers.title: Out of Context: Reliability in Multimodal Anomaly Detection Requires Contextual Inference
top_papers.abstract: This paper addresses the unreliability of standard anomaly detection methods that assume a single, unconditional model of "normal" behavior. The core method introduces **contextual inference** to distinguish genuine anomalies from variations in operating conditions, recognizing that an observation's normalcy is context-dependent. This approach leverages multimodal data to build a more robust and context-aware reference model, significantly improving assessment reliability in dynamic environments.

top_papers.title: MOONSHOT : A Framework for Multi-Objective Pruning of Vision and Large Language Models
top_papers.abstract: MOONSHOT is a framework that enhances post-training, one-shot model pruning by transforming single-objective methods into a multi-objective optimization. It jointly minimizes both the layer-wise reconstruction error and the second-order Taylor approximation of the training loss, addressing the inconsistency of using either objective alone. This wrapper approach provides a general and scalable way to improve pruning effectiveness across various model architectures and sparsity levels.

top_papers.title: Text-Attributed Knowledge Graph Enrichment with Large Language Models for Medical Concept Representation
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **CoMed**, an LLM-empowered framework to enrich medical knowledge graphs (KGs) for better medical concept representation. CoMed addresses limitations in existing KGs by integrating statistically derived, clinically relevant cross-type dependencies from EHRs with rich semantic information extracted from clinical text using Large Language Models. This dual integration results in higher-quality concept embeddings crucial for downstream clinical prediction tasks.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of CoMed . (1) Extract intra-visit co-occurrence and next-visit transition evidence from EHRs, and retain well-supported code pairs. (2) Use type-constrained, evidence-conditioned LLM prompting to assign directed relation types (with confidence and rationales), thereby constructing a heterogeneous KG. (3) Enrich nodes with LLM-generated concept descriptions and edges with relation metadata. (4) Jointly train a LoRA-tuned LLaMA encoder and a relation-aware GNN to learn medical concept embeddings for downstream prediction.

top_papers.title: An Analysis of Regularization and Fokker-Planck Residuals in Diffusion Models for Image Generation
top_papers.abstract: This paper analyzes the trade-off between regularization techniques and the Fokker-Planck (FP) equation residuals in diffusion models for image generation. The authors empirically investigate lightweight regularizers as alternatives to directly penalizing FP deviations, which is computationally expensive. They demonstrate that simpler regularization methods can achieve similar benefits in reducing FP residuals and improving sample quality at a significantly lower computational cost.
top_papers.figure_caption: Noise injection in the forward process following ( 1 ) (top) and image generation from noise according to ( 4 ) (bottom).

top_papers.title: OmniLight: One Model to Rule All Lighting Conditions
top_papers.abstract: OmniLight is a generalized model designed to handle diverse adverse lighting conditions, including shadows and irregular illumination, by unifying multiple restoration tasks. Its core method involves extending the specialized DINOLight framework with a novel **Wavelet Domain Mixture-of-Experts (WD-MoE)** layer. This contribution enables OmniLight to be trained across all datasets simultaneously, achieving robust performance across various lighting domains rather than specializing in just one.

top_papers.title: UniDoc-RL: Coarse-to-Fine Visual RAG with Hierarchical Actions and Dense Rewards
top_papers.abstract: UniDoc-RL proposes a unified reinforcement learning framework for visual RAG, enabling a Large Vision-Language Model agent to sequentially acquire visual evidence. The core method uses a hierarchical action space to move from coarse document retrieval to fine-grained image selection and region cropping, effectively suppressing irrelevant information. This approach is trained end-to-end using a dense multi-reward scheme, contributing a more precise and context-aware visual information gathering process for complex reasoning.
top_papers.figure_caption: Three critical factors for Visual RAG. UniDoc-RL address these challenges through the (a) Precise Selection action to bridge the semantic gap between coarse retrieval and reasoning, and an (b) Active Visual Perception action to focus on information-dense regions, both optimized via a (c) Dense multi-reward mechanism.

top_papers.title: Why Do Vision Language Models Struggle To Recognize Human Emotions?
top_papers.abstract: This paper investigates why Vision-Language Models (VLMs) struggle with dynamic facial emotion recognition (DFER). The authors find two key vulnerabilities: VLM pre-training exacerbates the long-tail distribution of emotion datasets, causing rare emotions to be misclassified as common ones. Their contribution is proposing alternative sampling strategies to mitigate this head-class bias.
top_papers.figure_caption: Qualitative Failure Analysis of SOTA VLMs on Video Emotion Recognition. VLMs like Gemini2.5-Flash, Qwen2.5-VL and EmotionQwen misclassify emotions by failing to capture subtle, temporal cues. Examples include overlooking a brief smile (top row, ‘Happiness’ misclassified as ‘Sadness/Neutral/Disgust’) or a tensed facial expression (bottom row, ‘Anger’ misclassified as ‘Neutral/Anxiety’)

top_papers.title: An Analysis of Regularization and Fokker-Planck Residuals in Diffusion Models for Image Generation
top_papers.abstract: This paper analyzes the trade-off between regularization techniques and the Fokker-Planck (FP) equation residuals in diffusion models for image generation. The authors empirically investigate lightweight penalty terms as alternatives to costly direct FP regularization. They demonstrate that simpler regularizers can achieve similar benefits in reducing FP residuals and improving sample quality at a substantially lower computational cost.
top_papers.figure_caption: Noise injection in the forward process following ( 1 ) (top) and image generation from noise according to ( 4 ) (bottom).

top_papers.title: OmniLight: One Model to Rule All Lighting Conditions
top_papers.abstract: OmniLight is a unified model designed to handle diverse adverse lighting conditions, including shadows and irregular illumination, by integrating two strategies. It extends the specialized ALN baseline, DINOLight, by incorporating a novel **Wavelet Domain Mixture-of-Experts (WD-MoE)** architecture. This allows OmniLight to be trained across multiple datasets simultaneously, achieving robust, generalized performance across various lighting domains.

top_papers.title: UniDoc-RL: Coarse-to-Fine Visual RAG with Hierarchical Actions and Dense Rewards
top_papers.abstract: UniDoc-RL introduces a unified reinforcement learning framework for visual RAG, enabling a Large Vision-Language Model agent to sequentially acquire visual evidence through coarse-to-fine steps. The method employs a hierarchical action space for progressive refinement, moving from document retrieval to fine-grained region cropping, guided by a dense, task-aware multi-reward scheme. This approach allows the model to suppress irrelevant content and focus on information-dense visual regions for complex reasoning.
top_papers.figure_caption: Three critical factors for Visual RAG. UniDoc-RL address these challenges through the (a) Precise Selection action to bridge the semantic gap between coarse retrieval and reasoning, and an (b) Active Visual Perception action to focus on information-dense regions, both optimized via a (c) Dense multi-reward mechanism.

top_papers.title: Why Do Vision Language Models Struggle To Recognize Human Emotions?
top_papers.abstract: This paper investigates why Vision-Language Models (VLMs) fail at recognizing human emotions, identifying two core vulnerabilities. The authors find that the long-tailed nature of emotion datasets, amplified by VLM pre-training, causes models to collapse rare emotions into common categories. Their contribution involves proposing alternative sampling strategies to mitigate this head-class bias.
top_papers.figure_caption: Qualitative Failure Analysis of SOTA VLMs on Video Emotion Recognition. VLMs like Gemini2.5-Flash, Qwen2.5-VL and EmotionQwen misclassify emotions by failing to capture subtle, temporal cues. Examples include overlooking a brief smile (top row, ‘Happiness’ misclassified as ‘Sadness/Neutral/Disgust’) or a tensed facial expression (bottom row, ‘Anger’ misclassified as ‘Neutral/Anxiety’)

top_papers.title: AMO-ENE: Attention-based Multi-Omics Fusion Model for Outcome Prediction in Extra Nodal Extension and HPV-associated Oropharyngeal Cancer
top_papers.abstract: Extranodal extension (ENE) is an emerging prognostic factor in human papillomavirus (HPV)-associated oropharyngeal cancer (OPC), although it is currently omitted as a clinical staging criteria. Recent works have advocated for the inclusion of iENE as a prognostic marker in HPV-positive OPC staging. However, several practical limitations continue to hinder its clinical integration, including inconsistencies in segmentation, low contrast in the periphery of metastatic lymph nodes on CT imaging, and laborious manual annotations. To address these limitations, we propose a fully automated end-to-end pipeline that uses computed tomography (CT) images with clinical data to assess the status of nodal ENE and predict treatment outcomes. Our approach includes a hierarchical 3D semi-supervised segmentation model designed to detect and delineate relevant iENE from radiotherapy planning CT scans. From these segmentations, a set of radiomics and deep features are extracted to train an imaging-detected ENE grading classifier. The predicted ENE status is then evaluated for its prognostic value and compared with existing staging criteria. Furthermore, we integrate these nodal features with primary tumor characteristics in a multimodal, attention-based outcome prediction model, providing a dynamic framework for outcome prediction. Our method is validated in an internal cohort of 397 HPV-positive OPC patients treated with radiation therapy or chemoradiotherapy between 2009 and 2020. For outcome prediction at the 2-year mark, our pipeline surpassed baseline models with 88.2% (4.8) in AUC for metastatic recurrence, 79.2% (7.4) for overall survival, and 78.1% (8.6) for disease-free survival. We also obtain a concordance index of 83.3% (6.5) for metastatic recurrence, 71.3% (8.9) for overall survival, and 70.0% (8.1) for disease-free survival, making it feasible for clinical decision making.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出了一种名为 **AMO-ENE** 的端到端多组学融合模型，用于预测 HPV 相关口咽癌的治疗结局。它结合了**基于注意力的 3D 图像分割**来自动识别和勾勒淋巴结外侵犯（ENE），并融合影像组学和深度特征，以克服手动分割的限制。
top_papers.figure_caption: Illustration for iENE status grading system. Nodes are presented with white borders, red healthy tissue background and black tumoral content. Grade 0 indicates metastatic pathological node with no signs of extension. Grade 1 indicates extension through the nodal capsule into surrounding fat. Grade 2 characterizes coalescent nodal masses with loss of internodal planes. Grade 3 is defined by overt invasion of surrounding structures and tissues. In case of multiple ENE, the maximum grade is reported.

top_papers.title: EGLOCE: Training-Free Energy-Guided Latent Optimization for Concept Erasure
top_papers.abstract: As text-to-image diffusion models grow increasingly prevalent, the ability to remove specific concepts-mostly explicit content and many copyrighted characters or styles-has become essential for safety and compliance. Existing unlearning approaches often require costly re-training, modify parameters at the cost of degradation of unrelated concept fidelity, or depend on indirect inference-time adjustment that compromise the effectiveness of concept erasure. Inspired by the success of energy-guided sampling for preservation of the condition of diffusion models, we introduce Energy-Guided Latent Optimization for Concept Erasure (EGLOCE), a training-free approach that removes unwanted concepts by re-directing noisy latent during inference. Our method employs a dual-objective framework: a repulsion energy that steers generation away from target concepts via gradient descent in latent space, and a retention energy that preserves semantic alignment to the original prompt. Combined with previous approaches that either require erroneous modified model weights or provide weak inference-time guidance, EGLOCE operates entirely at inference and enhances erasure performance, enabling plug-and-play integration. Extensive experiments demonstrate that EGLOCE improves concept removal while maintaining image quality and prompt alignment across baselines, even with adversarial attacks. To the best of our knowledge, our work is the first to establish a new paradigm for safe and controllable image generation through dual energy-based guidance during sampling.
top_papers.abstract_zh: EGLOCE提出了一种**无需训练**的方法，通过在推理时**引导潜在空间**来实现概念擦除。它利用**排斥能量**使生成远离目标概念，同时用**保持能量**确保与原提示的语义一致性。该方法有效且避免了重新训练或损害其他概念保真度的问题。

top_papers.title: Structure-Aware Fine-Grained Gaussian Splatting for Expressive Avatar Reconstruction
top_papers.abstract: Reconstructing photorealistic and topology-aware human avatars from monocular videos remains a significant challenge in the fields of computer vision and graphics. While existing 3D human avatar modeling approaches can effectively capture body motion, they often fail to accurately model fine details such as hand movements and facial expressions. To address this, we propose Structure-aware Fine-grained Gaussian Splatting (SFGS), a novel method for reconstructing expressive and coherent full-body 3D human avatars from a monocular video sequence. The SFGS use both spatial-only triplane and time-aware hexplane to capture dynamic features across consecutive frames. A structure-aware gaussian module is designed to capture pose-dependent details in a spatially coherent manner and improve pose and texture expression. To better model hand deformations, we also propose a residual refinement module based on fine-grained hand reconstruction. Our method requires only a single-stage training and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in both quantitative and qualitative evaluations, generating high-fidelity avatars with natural motion and fine details. The code is on Github: https://github.com/Su245811YZ/SFGS
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出了**结构感知细粒度高斯泼溅（SFGS）**方法，利用空间三平面和时间六平面捕捉动态特征。其核心在于设计了**结构感知高斯模块**以实现空间一致的姿态依赖细节捕捉，并引入**残差细化模块**以精确建模手部变形，从而从单目视频重建出逼真且拓扑一致的表情丰富的人体化身。
top_papers.figure_caption: Limitation of conventional human gaussian splatting methods. The second row shows the error between the reconstructed 3D avatar from the corresponding input image in the first row and the 3D ground truth, with darker colors indicating larger reconstruction errors. The hand exhibits the largest reconstruction error across the entire body.

top_papers.title: Through Their Eyes: Fixation-aligned Tuning for Personalized User Emulation
top_papers.abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed as scalable user simulators for recommender system evaluation. Yet existing simulators perceive recommendations through text or structured metadata rather than the visual interfaces real users browse-a critical gap, since attention over recommendation layouts is both visually driven and highly personalized. We investigate whether aligning a vision-language model's (VLM's) visual attention with user-specific gaze patterns can improve simulation fidelity. Analysis of a real-world eye-tracking dataset collected in a carousel-based recommendation setting reveals that users exhibit stable individual gaze patterns strongly predictive of click behavior. Building on this finding, we propose Fixation-Aligned Tuning for user Emulation (FixATE). Our approach first probes the VLM's internal visual attention via interpretability operators to obtain a slot-level relevance distribution comparable with human fixation, and then learns personalized soft prompts to steer the model's attention toward each user's characteristic fixation pattern. Experiments across three interpretability-based probing operators and two architecturally distinct VLM backbones demonstrate consistent improvements in both attention alignment and click prediction accuracy. These results suggest that making the model "see like the user" is a viable path toward simulators that more faithfully reproduce how users perceive and act in recommendation interfaces.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出**FixATE**方法，通过将**视觉-语言模型（VLM）的内部视觉注意力与用户的真实眼动模式对齐**，来提升推荐系统用户模拟的保真度。核心贡献在于证明了**基于个体凝视模式的微调**能有效捕捉用户对推荐布局的视觉偏好，从而更准确地模拟用户行为。
top_papers.figure_caption: Figure 1 . The difference of the perception of recommendations between real users and LLM-based user simulators.

top_papers.title: VAG: Dual-Stream Video-Action Generation for Embodied Data Synthesis
top_papers.abstract: Recent advances in robot foundation models trained on large-scale human teleoperation data have enabled robots to perform increasingly complex real-world tasks. However, scaling these systems remains difficult because collecting task-specific demonstrations is expensive and labor-intensive. Synthetic data, especially generated videos, offer a promising direction, but existing World Models (WMs) are not directly suitable for policy learning since they do not provide paired action trajectories. World-Action (WA) models partially address this by predicting actions with visual outputs, yet often lack strong video-action alignment, while two-stage pipelines that generate video first and then infer actions introduce inefficiency and error accumulation. To address these limitations, we propose VAG, a unified flow-matching-based dual-stream framework that jointly generates video and action under visual and language conditioning. By synchronizing denoising in both branches and using an adaptive 3D pooling mechanism to transfer compact global video context to the action branch, VAG improves cross-modal consistency during generation. Across both simulated and real-world settings, VAG produces aligned video-action pairs with competitive prediction quality, supports executable trajectory replay, and provides useful synthetic pretraining data that improves downstream policy generalization, indicating its potential as a practical world-action model for embodied data synthesis.
top_papers.abstract_zh: VAG 提出了一种基于流匹配的双流框架，用于联合生成视频和动作，以解决机器人学习中合成数据对齐和效率问题。它通过同步视频和动作的去噪过程，实现了更强的视频-动作对齐，为具身智能体提供了高质量的合成数据。
top_papers.figure_caption: Illustration and capabilities of VAG. (a) We train our dual-stream video-action generation model using teleoperated robot trajectories. (b) Given an initial frame and a language instruction, the model can synchronously generate aligned video–action data pairs. (c) The generated data can be used to train robot policies for improved generalization. (d) The actions generated by the model can also be applied for real-world robot replay. The successful task execution demonstrates that the model holds the potential to function as a policy.

top_papers.title: VAGNet: Vision-based accident anticipation with global features
top_papers.abstract: Traffic accidents are a leading cause of fatalities and injuries across the globe. Therefore, the ability to anticipate hazardous situations in advance is essential. Automated accident anticipation enables timely intervention through driver alerts and collision avoidance maneuvers, forming a key component of advanced driver assistance systems. In autonomous driving, such predictive capabilities support proactive safety behaviors, such as initiating defensive driving and human takeover when required. Using dashcam video as input offers a cost-effective solution, but it is challenging due to the complexity of real-world driving scenes. Accident anticipation systems need to operate in real-time. However, current methods involve extracting features from each detected object, which is computationally intensive. We propose VAGNet, a deep neural network that learns to predict accidents from dash-cam video using global features of traffic scenes without requiring explicit object-level features. The network consists of transformer and graph modules, and we use the vision foundation model VideoMAE-V2 for global feature extraction. Experiments on four benchmark datasets (DAD, DoTA, DADA, and Nexar) show that our method anticipates accidents with higher average precision and mean time-to-accident while being computationally more efficient compared to existing methods.
top_papers.abstract_zh: VAGNet 提出了一种基于深度学习的方法，利用**全局场景特征**而非耗时的逐对象特征来预测交通事故。其核心贡献在于实现了**高效的、基于视频的事故预警**，适用于实时自动驾驶系统。
top_papers.figure_caption: Architecture of the proposed VAGNet accident anticipation framework.

top_papers.title: Anthropogenic Regional Adaptation in Multimodal Vision-Language Model
top_papers.abstract: While the field of vision-language (VL) has achieved remarkable success in integrating visual and textual information across multiple languages and domains, there is still no dedicated framework for assessing human-centric alignment in vision-language systems. We offer two contributions to address this gap. First, we introduce Anthropogenic Regional Adaptation: a novel paradigm that aims to optimize model relevance to specific regional contexts while ensuring the retention of global generalization capabilities. Second, we present a simple, but effective adaptation method named Geographical-generalization-made-easy (GG-EZ), which utilizes regional data filtering and model merging. Through comprehensive experiments on 3 VL architectures: large vision-language models, text-to-image diffusion models, and vision-language embedding models, and a case study in Southeast Asia (SEA) regional adaptation, we demonstrate the importance of Anthropogenic Regional Adaptation and the effectiveness of GG-EZ, showing 5-15% gains in cultural relevance metrics across SEA while maintaining over 98% of global performance and even occasionally surpassing it. Our findings establish Anthropogenic Regional Alignment as a foundational paradigm towards applicability of multimodal vision-language models in diverse regions and demonstrate a simple-yet-effective baseline method that optimizes regional value alignment while preserving global generalization.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出了**人类区域适应（Anthropogenic Regional Adaptation）**的新范式，旨在优化多模态模型对特定区域的关联性，同时保持全局泛化能力。作者还提出了**GG-EZ**方法，通过区域数据过滤和模型合并来实现这种适应。
top_papers.figure_caption: Through anthropogenic regional adaptation, we identify two primary model archetypes: (left) Global model with strong overall global performance, but struggle to represent certain regions appropriately, and (right) Regional-specific model that has a strong representation towards certain regions, but fall short on the global context.

top_papers.title: EgoFun3D: Modeling Interactive Objects from Egocentric Videos using Function Templates
top_papers.abstract: We present EgoFun3D, a coordinated task formulation, dataset, and benchmark for modeling interactive 3D objects from egocentric videos. Interactive objects are of high interest for embodied AI but scarce, making modeling from readily available real-world videos valuable. Our task focuses on obtaining simulation-ready interactive 3D objects from egocentric video input. While prior work largely focuses on articulations, we capture general cross-part functional mappings (e.g., rotation of stove knob controls stove burner temperature) through function templates, a structured computational representation. Function templates enable precise evaluation and direct compilation into executable code across simulation platforms. To enable comprehensive benchmarking, we introduce a dataset of 271 egocentric videos featuring challenging real-world interactions with paired 3D geometry, segmentation over 2D and 3D, articulation and function template annotations. To tackle the task, we propose a 4-stage pipeline consisting of: 2D part segmentation, reconstruction, articulation estimation, and function template inference. Comprehensive benchmarking shows that the task is challenging for off-the-shelf methods, highlighting avenues for future work.
top_papers.abstract_zh: EgoFun3D 提出了一种新颖的框架，用于从第一人称视频中建模可交互的 3D 物体，重点在于捕获跨部件的功能映射。其核心方法是引入**函数模板**（Function Templates）作为结构化表示，以精确描述和编译物体的交互功能。该工作贡献了一个新的**数据集和基准**，推动了具身AI对真实世界交互物体的理解和模拟。
top_papers.figure_caption: We present EgoFun3D : a coordinated task, dataset and benchmark for modeling interactive 3D objects from egocentric videos. Given an egocentric video as an input, the output is a simulation-ready interactive object (e.g., faucet handle starts water flow from faucet spout). We break down the task into 4 steps to propose a baseline approach using off-the-shelf components. Our function template representation, produced by the proposed system, can be compiled into executable code for a simulator of choice.

top_papers.title: Hierarchical Textual Knowledge for Enhanced Image Clustering
top_papers.abstract: Image clustering aims to group images in an unsupervised fashion. Traditional methods focus on knowledge from visual space, making it difficult to distinguish between visually similar but semantically different classes. Recent advances in vision-language models enable the use of textual knowledge to enhance image clustering. However, most existing methods rely on coarse class labels or simple nouns, overlooking the rich conceptual and attribute-level semantics embedded in textual space. In this paper, we propose a knowledge-enhanced clustering (KEC) method that constructs a hierarchical concept-attribute structured knowledge with the help of large language models (LLMs) to guide clustering. Specifically, we first condense redundant textual labels into abstract concepts and then automatically extract discriminative attributes for each single concept and similar concept pairs, via structured prompts to LLMs. This knowledge is instantiated for each input image to achieve the knowledge-enhanced features. The knowledge-enhanced features with original visual features are adapted to various downstream clustering algorithms. We evaluate KEC on 20 diverse datasets, showing consistent improvements across existing methods using additional textual knowledge. KEC without training outperforms zero-shot CLIP on 14 out of 20 datasets. Furthermore, the naive use of textual knowledge may harm clustering performance, while KEC provides both accuracy and robustness.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出了一种知识增强聚类（KEC）方法，利用大型语言模型（LLM）构建了**层次化的概念-属性结构知识**来指导图像聚类。核心方法是**提炼抽象概念并自动提取概念间的判别性属性**，以克服仅依赖视觉信息或粗粒度文本标签的局限性。这使得模型能更好地利用丰富的文本语义来区分语义不同但视觉相似的图像。
top_papers.figure_caption: Motivation and intuition behind KEC. We first contrast the type of knowledge used by existing methods versus our method. It also illustrates that class labels alone often fail to identify objects of similar categories. Building on prior work showing that CLIP attends to targeted semantical regions [ 40 ] , we visualize the attention maps derived from nouns and discriminative attributes using the method from [ 4 ] . The results motivate us to mine discriminative attributes to construct structured textual knowledge for guiding image clustering.

top_papers.title: HuiYanEarth-SAR: A Foundation Model for High-Fidelity and Low-Cost Global Remote Sensing Imagery Generation
top_papers.abstract: Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) imagery generation is essential for deepening the study of scattering mechanisms, establishing trustworthy electromagnetic scene models, and fundamentally alleviating the data scarcity bottleneck that constrains development in this field. However, existing methods find it difficult to simultaneously ensure high fidelity in both global geospatial semantics and microscopic scattering mechanisms, resulting in severe challenges for global generation. To address this, we propose \textbf{HuiYanEarth-SAR}, the first foundational SAR imagery generation model based on AlphaEarth and integrated scattering mechanisms. By injecting geospatial priors to control macroscopic structures and utilizing implicit scattering characteristic modeling to ensure the authenticity of microscopic textures, we achieve the capability of generating high-fidelity SAR images for global locations solely based on geographic coordinates. This study not only constructs an efficient SAR scene simulator but also establishes a bridge connecting geography, scatter mechanism, and artificial intelligence from a methodological standpoint. It advances SAR research by expanding the paradigm from perception and understanding to simulation and creation, providing key technical support for constructing a high-confidence digital twin of the Earth.
top_papers.abstract_zh:
**核心方法：** HuiYanEarth-SAR是一个基于AlphaEarth的SAR图像生成基础模型，通过注入地理空间先验控制宏观结构，并利用隐式散射特性建模确保微观纹理真实性。

**核心贡献：** 首次实现了仅基于地理坐标，就能生成高保真、低成本的全球SAR图像，有效缓解了数据稀缺问题。
top_papers.figure_caption: (a) We propose HuiYanEarth-SAR, the first generative foundation model conditioned on both geophysical priors and SAR scattering mechanism. The model only takes the geographic coordinates as input with low cost, ultimately generating high-fidelity SAR images that are strictly aligned with the input conditions and physically consistent. (b) HuiYanEarth-SAR shows powerful capabilities in generating global, high-fidelity SAR images. Code is available at https://github.com/JoyeZLearning/HuiYanEarth-SAR .

top_papers.title: Improving Layout Representation Learning Across Inconsistently Annotated Datasets via Agentic Harmonization
top_papers.abstract: Fine-tuning object detection (OD) models on combined datasets assumes annotation compatibility, yet datasets often encode conflicting spatial definitions for semantically equivalent categories. We propose an agentic label harmonization workflow that uses a vision-language model to reconcile both category semantics and bounding box granularity across heterogeneous sources before training. We evaluate on document layout detection as a challenging case study, where annotation standards vary widely across corpora. Without harmonization, naïve mixed-dataset fine-tuning degrades a pretrained RT-DETRv2 detector: on SCORE-Bench, which measures how accurately the full document conversion pipeline reproduces ground-truth structure, table TEDS drops from 0.800 to 0.750. Applied to two corpora whose 16 and 10 category taxonomies share only 8 direct correspondences, harmonization yields consistent gains across content fidelity, table structure, and spatial consistency: detection F-score improves from 0.860 to 0.883, table TEDS improves to 0.814, and mean bounding box overlap drops from 0.043 to 0.016. Representation analysis further shows that harmonized training produces more compact and separable post-decoder embeddings, confirming that annotation inconsistency distorts the learned feature space and that resolving it before training restores representation structure.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出了一种**代理式标签协调工作流**，利用视觉-语言模型来调和不同数据集中类别语义和边界框粒度的不一致性。其核心贡献在于，通过在模型训练前进行数据异构性对齐，**显著提高了跨不一致标注数据集的布局检测性能**，解决了简单混合训练导致的性能下降问题。

top_papers.title: LARY: A Latent Action Representation Yielding Benchmark for Generalizable Vision-to-Action Alignment
top_papers.abstract: While the shortage of explicit action data limits Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models, human action videos offer a scalable yet unlabeled data source. A critical challenge in utilizing large-scale human video datasets lies in transforming visual signals into ontology-independent representations, known as latent actions. However, the capacity of latent action representation to derive robust control from visual observations has yet to be rigorously evaluated. We introduce the Latent Action Representation Yielding (LARY) Benchmark, a unified framework for evaluating latent action representations on both high-level semantic actions (what to do) and low-level robotic control (how to do). The comprehensively curated dataset encompasses over one million videos (1,000 hours) spanning 151 action categories, alongside 620K image pairs and 595K motion trajectories across diverse embodiments and environments. Our experiments reveal two crucial insights: (i) General visual foundation models, trained without any action supervision, consistently outperform specialized embodied latent action models. (ii) Latent-based visual space is fundamentally better aligned to physical action space than pixel-based space. These results suggest that general visual representations inherently encode action-relevant knowledge for physical control, and that semantic-level abstraction serves as a fundamentally more effective pathway from vision to action than pixel-level reconstruction.
top_papers.abstract_zh:
该论文提出了**LARY基准**，用于统一评估**潜在动作表示**在**高层语义动作**和**底层机器人控制**任务中的泛化能力。

核心方法是利用海量无标签的人类动作视频，将视觉信号转化为**本体论无关的潜在动作表示**，并首次对其在VLA任务中的鲁棒性进行了严格检验。
top_papers.figure_caption: LARYBench evaluates vision-to-action transformation on both action generalization and robotic control.

top_papers.title: Panoptic Pairwise Distortion Graph
top_papers.abstract: In this work, we introduce a new perspective on comparative image assessment by representing an image pair as a structured composition of its regions. In contrast, existing methods focus on whole image analysis, while implicitly relying on region-level understanding. We extend the intra-image notion of a scene graph to inter-image, and propose a novel task of Distortion Graph (DG). DG treats paired images as a structured topology grounded in regions, and represents dense degradation information such as distortion type, severity, comparison and quality score in a compact interpretable graph structure. To realize the task of learning a distortion graph, we contribute (i) a region-level dataset, PandaSet, (ii) a benchmark suite, PandaBench, with varying region-level difficulty, and (iii) an efficient architecture, Panda, to generate distortion graphs. We demonstrate that PandaBench poses a significant challenge for state-of-the-art multimodal large language models (MLLMs) as they fail to understand region-level degradations even when fed with explicit region cues. We show that training on PandaSet or prompting with DG elicits region-wise distortion understanding, opening a new direction for fine-grained, structured pairwise image assessment.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出**失真图（Distortion Graph, DG）**，将图像对表示为基于区域的结构化拓扑，以捕捉密集的失真信息。为实现此任务，研究贡献了**PandaSet数据集**、**PandaBench基准**和**Panda模型**，旨在超越传统全图评估。
top_papers.figure_caption: DG Task Overview. Top: Given two images, Panda learns the proposed Distortion Graph ( DG ). Bottom: Grounded Subgraphs illustrate how DG grounds regions in terms of distortion information.

top_papers.title: Seg2Change: Adapting Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation Model for Remote Sensing Change Detection
top_papers.abstract: Change detection is a fundamental task in remote sensing, aiming to quantify the impacts of human activities and ecological dynamics on land-cover changes. Existing change detection methods are limited to predefined classes in training datasets, which constrains their scalability in real-world scenarios. In recent years, numerous advanced open-vocabulary semantic segmentation models have emerged for remote sensing imagery. However, there is still a lack of an effective framework for directly applying these models to open-vocabulary change detection (OVCD), a novel task that integrates vision and language to detect changes across arbitrary categories. To address these challenges, we first construct a category-agnostic change detection dataset, termed CA-CDD. Further, we design a category-agnostic change head to detect the transitions of arbitrary categories and index them to specific classes. Based on them, we propose Seg2Change, an adapter designed to adapt open-vocabulary semantic segmentation models to change detection task. Without bells and whistles, this simple yet effective framework achieves state-of-the-art OVCD performance (+9.52 IoU on WHU-CD and +5.50 mIoU on SECOND). Our code is released at https://github.com/yogurts-sy/Seg2Change.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出 **Seg2Change** 框架，旨在将开放词汇语义分割模型应用于遥感影像的开放词汇变化检测（OVCD）。核心方法是设计了一个**类别无关的变化检测头**，用于检测任意类别的变化并进行索引，同时构建了**类别无关的变化检测数据集（CA-CDD）**。主要贡献在于首次有效适配了开放词汇模型到OVCD任务，提高了变化检测的泛化能力。
top_papers.figure_caption: Figure 1. Previous paradigms vs. our paradigm. Previous paradigms rely on change proposals and are constrained by the segmentation performance of the proposal generator. Meanwhile, both paradigms (a–b) distinguish changed instances using a predefined threshold, which makes it difficult to accurately delineate change boundaries. In contrast, our paradigm, based on category-agnostic change maps, can effectively leverage more powerful open-vocabulary segmentation models.

top_papers.title: Sign Language Recognition in the Age of LLMs
top_papers.abstract: Recent Vision Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of multimodal reasoning tasks. This raises the question of whether such general-purpose models can also address specialized visual recognition problems such as isolated sign language recognition (ISLR) without task-specific training. In this work, we investigate the capability of modern VLMs to perform ISLR in a zero-shot setting. We evaluate several open-source and proprietary VLMs on the WLASL300 benchmark. Our experiments show that, under prompt-only zero-shot inference, current open-source VLMs remain far behind classic supervised ISLR classifiers by a wide margin. However, follow-up experiments reveal that these models capture partial visual-semantic alignment between signs and text descriptions. Larger proprietary models achieve substantially higher accuracy, highlighting the importance of model scale and training data diversity. All our code is publicly available on GitHub.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文研究了**大型视觉语言模型（VLMs）在零样本设置下进行孤立手语识别（ISLR）的能力**。 结果显示，**开源模型性能远逊于传统方法，但大型专有模型表现出潜力，表明模型规模和数据多样性的重要性**。
top_papers.figure_caption: Evaluation paradigms for zero-shot ISLR with VLMs. Top: Standard closed-set classification with defined target labels. Middle: Zero-shot prediction instructing the model to behave as an ASL expert capable of categorizing the input videos into an open set. Bottom: Zero-shot binary classification instructing the model to think about the input video to conclude whether the performed sign is the one the user thinks of, with optional knowledge about the data.

top_papers.title: STS-Mixer: Spatio-Temporal-Spectral Mixer for 4D Point Cloud Video Understanding
top_papers.abstract: 4D point cloud videos capture rich spatial and temporal dynamics of scenes which possess unique values in various 4D understanding tasks. However, most existing methods work in the spatiotemporal domain where the underlying geometric characteristics of 4D point cloud videos are hard to capture, leading to degraded representation learning and understanding of 4D point cloud videos. We address the above challenge from a complementary spectral perspective. By transforming 4D point cloud videos into graph spectral signals, we can decompose them into multiple frequency bands each of which captures distinct geometric structures of point cloud videos. Our spectral analysis reveals that the decomposed low-frequency signals capture more coarse shapes while high-frequency signals encode more fine-grained geometry details. Building on these observations, we design Spatio-Temporal-Spectral Mixer (STS-Mixer), a unified framework that mixes spatial, temporal, and spectral representations of point cloud videos. STS-Mixer integrates multi-band delineated spectral signals with spatiotemporal information to capture rich geometries and temporal dynamics, while enabling fine-grained and holistic understanding of 4D point cloud videos. Extensive experiments show that STS-Mixer achieves superior performance consistently across multiple widely adopted benchmarks on both 3D action recognition and 4D semantic segmentation tasks. Code and models are available at https://github.com/Vegetebird/STS-Mixer.
top_papers.abstract_zh: STS-Mixer提出了一种新颖的**时空-谱混合（Spatio-Temporal-Spectral Mixer）**框架，通过将4D点云视频转换为**图谱信号**并进行**多频带分解**，以捕捉其独特的几何特征。该方法**融合了空间、时间与不同频率的谱信息**，有效克服了纯时空方法难以捕捉几何细节的局限，提升了4D点云视频的理解能力。
top_papers.figure_caption: Graph spectral analysis of point clouds with Graph Fourier Transform (GFT) and inverse GFT (IGFT). The high-frequency bands of point clouds encode fine-grained geometric details which can be observed by the difference between the original point cloud and the one w/o high frequency. The low-frequency bands capture most energy as well as the coarse shapes which can be observed by the similarity between the original point cloud and the one w/o mid-high frequency.

top_papers.title: Who Handles Orientation? Investigating Invariance in Feature Matching
top_papers.abstract: Finding matching keypoints between images is a core problem in 3D computer vision. However, modern matchers struggle with large in-plane rotations. A straightforward mitigation is to learn rotation invariance via data augmentation. However, it remains unclear at which stage rotation invariance should be incorporated. In this paper, we study this in the context of a modern sparse matching pipeline. We perform extensive experiments by training on a large collection of 3D vision datasets and evaluating on popular image matching benchmarks. Surprisingly, we find that incorporating rotation invariance already in the descriptor yields similar performance to handling it in the matcher. However, rotation invariance is achieved earlier in the matcher when it is learned in the descriptor, allowing for a faster rotation-invariant matcher. Further, we find that enforcing rotation invariance does not hurt upright performance when trained at scale. Finally, we study the emergence of rotation invariance through scale and find that increasing the training data size substantially improves generalization to rotated images. We release two matchers robust to in-plane rotations that achieve state-of-the-art performance on e.g. multi-modal (WxBS), extreme (HardMatch), and satellite image matching (SatAst). Code is available at https://github.com/davnords/loma.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文研究了在特征匹配中引入旋转不变性的最佳阶段。**核心方法**是通过在描述符或匹配器中学习旋转不变性，并在大规模数据集上进行实验比较。**主要贡献**在于发现将不变性融入描述符即可获得与在匹配器中学习相似的性能，且能加速匹配过程，同时不损害直立图像的性能。

top_papers.title: Dynamic Summary Generation for Interpretable Multimodal Depression Detection
top_papers.abstract: Depression remains widely underdiagnosed and undertreated because stigma and subjective symptom ratings hinder reliable screening. To address this challenge, we propose a coarse-to-fine, multi-stage framework that leverages large language models (LLMs) for accurate and interpretable detection. The pipeline performs binary screening, five-class severity classification, and continuous regression. At each stage, an LLM produces progressively richer clinical summaries that guide a multimodal fusion module integrating text, audio, and video features, yielding predictions with transparent rationale. The system then consolidates all summaries into a concise, human-readable assessment report. Experiments on the E-DAIC and CMDC datasets show significant improvements over state-of-the-art baselines in both accuracy and interpretability.
top_papers.abstract_zh: 该论文提出一个**粗粒到细粒的多阶段框架**，利用大型语言模型（LLMs）生成**递进的临床摘要**来指导**多模态特征融合**，从而实现抑郁症的**高精度和可解释性检测**。其核心贡献在于通过LLM驱动的摘要生成，实现了**透明的预测推理**和**简洁的评估报告**。

top_papers.title: Synthius-Mem: Brain-Inspired Hallucination-Resistant Persona Memory Achieving 94.4% Memory Accuracy and 99.6% Adversarial Robustness on LoCoMo
top_papers.abstract: Providing AI agents with reliable long-term memory that does not hallucinate remains an open problem. Current approaches to memory for LLM agents -- sliding windows, summarization, embedding-based RAG, and flat fact extraction -- each reduce token cost but introduce catastrophic information loss, semantic drift, or uncontrolled hallucination about the user. The structural reason is architectural: every published memory system on the LoCoMo benchmark treats conversation as a retrieval problem over raw or lightly summarized dialogue segments, and none reports adversarial robustness, the ability to refuse questions about facts the user never disclosed. We present Synthius-Mem, a brain-inspired structured persona memory system that takes a fundamentally different approach. Instead of retrieving what was said, Synthius-Mem extracts what is known about the person: a full persona extraction pipeline decomposes conversations into six cognitive domains (biography, experiences, preferences, social circle, work, psychometrics), consolidates and deduplicates per domain, and retrieves structured facts via CategoryRAG at 21.79 ms latency. On the LoCoMo benchmark (ACL 2024, 10 conversations, 1,813 questions), Synthius-Mem achieves 94.37% accuracy, exceeding all published systems including MemMachine (91.69%, adversarial score is not reported) and human performance (87.9 F1). Core memory fact accuracy reaches 98.64%. Adversarial robustness, the hallucination resistance metric that no competing system reports, reaches 99.55%. Synthius-Mem reduces token consumption by ~5x compared to full-context replay while achieving higher accuracy. Synthius-Mem achieves state-of-the-art results on LoCoMo and is, to our knowledge, the only persona memory system that both exceeds human-level performance and reports adversarial robustness.
top_papers.abstract_zh: Synthius-Mem提出了一种受大脑启发的结构化人格记忆系统，通过将对话分解为六个认知领域来提取关于用户的知识，而非简单检索。该方法有效抵抗了幻觉，并在LoCoMo基准测试中实现了高记忆准确率和强大的对抗鲁棒性。

top_papers.title: A Sanity Check on Composed Image Retrieval
top_papers.abstract: This paper addresses the limitations of existing Composed Image Retrieval (CIR) benchmarks, which suffer from query ambiguity. The core method introduces **FISD**, a new, precisely controlled benchmark generated using diffusion models to eliminate ambiguity and allow for accurate evaluation across six dimensions. Furthermore, the authors propose an **automatic multi-round agentic evaluation framework** to assess CIR models in a sequential, interactive setting.

top_papers.title: ARGOS: Who, Where, and When in Agentic Multi-Camera Person Search
top_papers.abstract: ARGOS introduces a novel benchmark and framework that reframes multi-camera person search as an interactive reasoning task where an agent must strategically ask questions and use spatial/temporal tools to locate a target. The core method relies on a Spatio-Temporal Topology Graph (STTG) to ground reasoning about camera transitions under information asymmetry and a limited turn budget. This work significantly contributes by providing a challenging, multi-track benchmark that tests complex planning and reasoning abilities beyond standard perception in person search.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview. Left: An ARGOS agent interacts with an ambiguous witness through multi-turn dialogue, combining appearance, spatial, and temporal queries. Right: The four-module agent architecture (Analyst → \( \to \) Planner → \( \to \) Interviewer → \( \to \) Interpreter) forms an observe-think-act loop over the evaluation environment.

top_papers.title: Relaxing Anchor-Frame Dominance for Mitigating Hallucinations in Video Large Language Models
top_papers.abstract: This paper addresses hallucinations in Video-LLMs by identifying and mitigating the tendency for the model to over-rely on a single "anchor frame" during generation, leading to temporally imbalanced evidence aggregation. The core method involves relaxing the dominance of this anchor frame, which is found to be a persistent, model-specific structural bias, thereby promoting a more balanced temporal evidence utilization to reduce hallucinations.

top_papers.title: StructDiff: A Structure-Preserving and Spatially Controllable Diffusion Model for Single-Image Generation
top_papers.abstract: StructDiff is a single-scale diffusion model for single-image generation that addresses limitations in structure preservation and spatial control. It introduces an **adaptive receptive field** module to maintain global and local image distributions, and incorporates **3D positional encoding** as a spatial prior for flexible control over the position and scale of generated content. This combination enables the synthesis of diverse images while accurately preserving the source image's structure.
top_papers.figure_caption: StructDiff enables diverse generation and controllable editing from a single training image. Top : Diverse generation at arbitrary scales. Middle : Controllable generation through PE-guided geometric editing and mask-guided fine-tuning. Bottom : Various applications without retraining. Results demonstrate effectiveness in structural preservation and spatial controllability.

top_papers.title: Towards Long-horizon Agentic Multimodal Search
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **LMM-Searcher**, a novel framework for long-horizon multimodal search agents designed to overcome context explosion and high token costs. The core method involves **offloading visual assets to an external file system and representing them with lightweight textual identifiers (UIDs)**, allowing for on-demand visual loading via a specialized fetch tool. This approach effectively manages heterogeneous information over extended tasks while preserving crucial visual signals.
top_papers.figure_caption: An illustration of LMM-Searcher. For simplicity, we employ simple strings as uids in this figure. In real implementation, we use URLs. This figure serves solely as a functional demonstration and does not represent any actual search results.

top_papers.title: ViLL-E: Video LLM Embeddings for Retrieval
top_papers.abstract: ViLL-E introduces a novel embedding generation mechanism within a unified VideoLLM architecture designed to enhance performance on video retrieval tasks where standard VideoLLMs struggle. The core method involves a three-stage training process combining generative and contrastive learning, enabling the model to dynamically adjust its processing time based on video complexity. This approach significantly improves performance across various video understanding tasks, particularly temporal localization and retrieval.

top_papers.title: Visual Preference Optimization with Rubric Rewards
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **rDPO (rubric-based Direct Preference Optimization)**, a novel framework that leverages instance-specific, checklist-style rubrics to generate high-quality preference data for multimodal tasks. By scoring responses against these detailed criteria, rDPO captures fine-grained quality differences better than traditional outcome-based signals. This method significantly improves the performance of reward models and enhances downstream task performance compared to existing preference optimization pipelines.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of rDPO. The top lane constructs a model-agnostic instruction-rubric pool by mining challenging seeds via model ensembles and drafting instance-specific rubrics with structured “essential” and “additional” criteria. The bottom lane achieves on-policy data curation and optimization by sampling responses from a target policy, scoring them via rubric-grounded judging, and mining preference pairs to drive iterative DPO.

top_papers.title: Coding-Free and Privacy-Preserving MCP Framework for Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS), which automates the complex clinical research workflow using Large Language Models (LLMs) orchestrated via a Model Context Protocol (MCP). The core contribution is a **coding-free, privacy-preserving framework** where sensitive data remains secured within the MCP server, allowing users to drive the entire research pipeline (planning, cohort construction, analysis) solely through natural language commands.
top_papers.figure_caption: Overview of Clinical Agentic Research Intelligence System (CARIS). Given a user intent, CARIS autonomously executes a multi-step clinical research workflow by interacting with agents, heterogeneous datasets, and various tools, while allowing user intervention. Users do not have direct access to the file or DB systems.

top_papers.title: Development, Evaluation, and Deployment of a Multi-Agent System for Thoracic Tumor Board
top_papers.abstract: This paper describes the development and deployment of an automated, multi-agent AI system to generate concise patient summaries for the Stanford Thoracic Tumor Board, replacing a manual process. The authors evaluated various automated summarization methods against physician gold standards and fact-based metrics, ultimately deploying the best-performing tool into routine clinical practice. A key contribution is demonstrating the successful integration of AI workflows into clinical settings and validating the use of LLMs as effective evaluation judges.

top_papers.title: Modality-Native Routing in Agent-to-Agent Networks: A Multimodal A2A Protocol Extension
top_papers.abstract: This paper introduces **MMA2A**, a protocol extension for Agent-to-Agent (A2A) networks that implements **modality-native routing**. This method routes multimodal signals (voice, image, text) directly in their original format based on agent capability declarations. The core contribution is demonstrating that this native routing significantly improves task accuracy (by 20 percentage points over text-bottleneck methods), but only when the receiving agent possesses the necessary multimodal reasoning capabilities.

top_papers.title: SpecBound: Adaptive Bounded Self-Speculation with Layer-wise Confidence Calibration
